info.title: browserhand
info.description: A reliable, deterministic web-automation API for AI agents. Managed headless-browser sessions with selector-free perception (accessibility snapshot + stable refs, page content, screenshots) and structured action primitives. No server-side LLM — the calling agent reasons; browserhand executes. Each endpoint maps cleanly to a single tool.

bearerAuth.description: Set only if BROWSERHAND_API_KEY is configured.

post.description: Opens an isolated headless browser session and returns its id plus a CDP connect URL.

schema.description: Options for a new browser session. All fields optional.

timeoutMs.description: Idle timeout for this session in ms. Defaults to the server setting.

context.description: Portable state from GET /v1/sessions/:id/context, to reuse cookies/localStorage (e.g. a logged-in session).

requestBody.description: Options for a new browser session. All fields optional.

429.description: Structured error. `code` is stable and machine-readable; `message` states how to recover.

get.summary: Accessibility snapshot with stable element refs
get.description: Returns the interactive/structural elements on the page, each with a `ref` used by the action endpoints, plus a readable outline. This is the selector-free basis for acting.

get.description: Returns the visible text of the page for the agent to read/extract. `truncated` signals the text was clipped.

get.description: Returns image/png so the agent can see the page. Use ?fullPage=true for the whole page or ?ref=eN for one element.

parameters.name: fullPage
parameters.description: Capture the full scrollable page. Default false (viewport only).
